title: Sub-team notify automations
excerpt: Automations to notify a sub-team when an action occurs!

posts.author: JustineBrice (Justine Brice)
posts.cookedHtml: Automations to notify a sub-team when an action occurs!

posts.author: connor_at_rally (Connor davis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

Thanks for your input. Your suggestion about automations to notify a sub-team when an action occurs is definitely a practical feature that would boost the overall efficiency of your team communication.

Monday.com has the capability to set up automations that can notify individual users or entire teams. However, it might not be as straightforward when it comes to notifying sub-teams.

Although this specific feature might not be readily available, our team of developers who specialize in creating custom integrations could potentially create a tailored solution for you. With a custom integration, we can set up more sophisticated notifications that can reach specific sub-teams based on your criteria.

We’d be delighted to discuss this further with you so we can better understand your needs and explore possible solutions. If this sounds interesting to you, let me know at connor@rallyessentials.com

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was unaware until stumbling across this request that it was not possible in monday to notify sub-teams!

I just finished redesigning standard corporate boards based upon sub-team permissioning, I am going to have to rethink that design.

monday, please add this capability!!

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @hbristol,

We developed an app called subitem automations that provides robust automation capabilities to subitems. Here’s an example of how it can be applied in this case →

Screenshot 2024-11-16 at 20.06.142058×260 21.3 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - https://monday.com/marketplace/10000287

Hope that helps!

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the suggestion @Fantasy-Media-Team but I am trying to send notifications to Sub-Teams, not send notifications on Sub-Items. If you are aware of a workaround on this please do let me know.

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml:
For what it is worth, it looks as though one workaround is to assign all of the sub-teams to the board directly, then you can send notifications to them.

Seems to take a lot of the power of sub-teams away though, as then you have to assign each sub-team to the board individually which is part of what I was trying to avoid in using sub-teams in the first place.
